_comment: CI-RUNNER baseline for .github/workflows/benchmark.yml's relative regression check. This is NOT the 60fps/reference-hardware target from PLAN.md (a 2022 MacBook Air M2 24GB and the developer's PC) -- GitHub Actions runners are different, variable-speed hardware; see tools/benchmark/README.md. Established from Performance benchmark workflow run https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/actions/runs/29160156520 on PR #755 (fix/refresh-benchmark-baseline), ubuntu-latest, benchmarks/scenarios/large-battle.json at scale 1x, warmup_ticks=60 measure_ticks=300 (the CI-tuned window in benchmark.yml, not BenchmarkRunner's local 120/600 defaults). Refreshes the prior baseline (2026-07-03, commit 2f4e984, PR #655), which had drifted ~52 commits stale and was flagging every current PR as a false regression (#748). To refresh: run the workflow, read the mean_ms/p95_ms it reports (workflow log or the PR comment it posts), and update the stats block below with a new _comment noting the new source run.
